message:
fix(tantivy): revert parallel cass document batch construction; stream messages directly into push_cass_document_into_pending so the max_chars/max_messages flush boundaries are honoured

Reverts the parallel-cass-document-construction work added in
`perf(indexer/tantivy): use rayon worker pool for lexical-rebuild
prep + grow Tantivy add-batch defaults`.  That change replaced
the streaming push-with-flush loop with a "build the full batch
in memory first, then iterate" pattern, which had a correctness
bug under large payloads:

  - The streaming form pushes each doc into a `docs: Vec` and a
    `pending_chars: usize` counter, then flushes the batch via
    `add_documents()` whenever `docs.len() >= max_messages` or
    `pending_chars >= max_chars` is reached.
  - The parallel form built `Vec<PreparedCassDocumentBatch>`
    materializing every doc in memory first, then drained it
    into the same pending vec.  That defeated the point of the
    flush boundary — by the time we reached the iteration we
    already had hundreds of MB of resident docs, far past the
    1 MB max_chars / 4096 max_messages caps the writer assumes.

Under workloads with many large messages (assistant responses
with ~2 KB content over 24-conversation × 256-msg batches), the
parallel form pinned the indexer at peak memory until the entire
batch finished, and on memory-pressured hosts the OOM killer
took the indexer instead of letting the streaming flush rescue
it.  Even on memory-rich hosts, the prep-then-flush sequencing
made the writer pool sit idle while the rayon prep pass
ran, which negated the parallelism benefit.

This commit goes back to the streaming form for both
`add_conversation` (single-conversation path) and
`add_conversations_with_ids` (multi-conversation path).
`cass_document_for_message(&context, msg)` is kept as the
per-message construction helper because it has no shared
mutable state and was useful for clarity, but it's now invoked
directly inside the per-message loop so each doc reaches
`push_cass_document_into_pending` immediately.

Removed: the `PreparedCassDocumentBatch`,
`build_cass_documents_for_message_slice_serial`,
`build_cass_documents_for_message_slice`, and
`build_cass_documents_for_conversations` helpers, plus the
`use rayon::prelude::*` import that they were the only
consumers of.

New regression test
-------------------
`add_conversations_with_ids_streams_large_payloads_without_dropping_docs`:
Builds 24 conversations × 256 messages × 2 KiB content (~12 MiB
total), calls `add_conversations_with_ids`, asserts every
single message lands as a Tantivy doc (`indexed_docs ==
expected_docs == 6_144`) and survives the `commit()` +
`reader().reload()` round trip.  Pre-revert this would either
OOM on small hosts or undercount on memory-pressured ones; with
the streaming form it passes deterministically.

Parallel cass-document prep is still on the table, but the next
attempt has to preserve the streaming flush invariant — likely
by parallelizing only within a single sub-batch that's already
sized to fit under max_chars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
